pkg/framework/entutils/testutils/ent1.purpose: Self-contained Ent codegen package whose `schema/` fixture (Example1) generates a standalone `db/` client used to test entutils transaction/mixin helpers (TransactingRepo, TxDriver, TimeMixin) in isolation from the production openmeter schema set.

patterns.name: Ignored-build codegen driver
patterns.description: entc.go is a `//go:build ignore` main package invoked by `go generate`; it is never compiled into the package.

patterns.name: Generate directive in package file
patterns.description: generate.go carries the lone `//go:generate go run -mod=mod entc.go` directive; it is the only real source file in the package proper.

patterns.name: Extension wiring stays in entc.go
patterns.description: Cursor/expose/paginate extensions are registered here so the generated db client supports the same features the production client tests rely on.

key_file_guides.role: Ent codegen entrypoint: targets `./db`, enables FeatureVersionedMigration/Lock/Upsert/ExecQuery, registers entcursor/entexpose/entpaginate extensions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must keep the `//go:build ignore` tag and the full extension list — ent1 is the richer fixture (includes entpaginate, unlike ent2). Dropping an extension silently removes generated methods the entutils tests depend on.

key_file_guides.role: Holds the `package ent1` declaration and the `//go:generate` directive that runs entc.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not move the directive elsewhere or hand-edit the generated `db/`; regenerate via `make generate` / `go generate`.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing the generated db/ client instead of changing schema/ and regenerating.
Removing entpaginate/entcursor/entexpose from the extension list when the entutils tests need that generated surface — ent1 intentionally carries the full set.
Compiling entc.go into the package by removing its `//go:build ignore` tag.

decisions.decision: Keep a tiny standalone Ent client separate from openmeter/ent.
decisions.rationale: Lets entutils test its transaction and mixin helpers without importing the large production schema or risking import cycles.

decisions.decision: ent1 is the feature-complete fixture (includes entpaginate).
decisions.rationale: Provides a client exercising cursor + pagination + expose so those entutils sub-packages can be validated; ent2 is the leaner counterpart.

pkg/framework/entutils/testutils/ent2.purpose: Second standalone Ent codegen package (Example2 schema → `db/` client) used alongside ent1 so entutils transaction helpers can be tested across two independent generated clients; deliberately leaner than ent1 (only entexpose, no entcursor/entpaginate).

patterns.name: Ignored-build codegen driver
patterns.description: entc.go is a `//go:build ignore` main invoked by `go generate`, mirroring ent1 but with a reduced extension set.

patterns.name: Generate directive in package file
patterns.description: generate.go holds the sole `//go:generate go run -mod=mod entc.go` directive under `package ent2`.

key_file_guides.role: Ent codegen entrypoint for the ent2 client: targets `./db`, enables FeatureVersionedMigration/Lock/Upsert/ExecQuery, registers only entexpose.New().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Intentionally omits entcursor/entpaginate (unlike ent1). Keep it minimal — this client exists to prove entutils works against a second, differently-configured generated client.

key_file_guides.role: `package ent2` declaration plus the `//go:generate` directive that runs entc.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Regenerate db/ via codegen; never hand-edit generated output.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing the generated ent2/db package instead of editing schema/ and regenerating.
Adding entcursor/entpaginate here to match ent1 — the divergence is intentional; ent2 is the lean fixture.
Removing the `//go:build ignore` tag from entc.go and pulling it into the package build.

decisions.decision: Maintain ent2 as a deliberately smaller second client.
decisions.rationale: Two distinct generated clients let entutils verify transaction/mixin behavior is not coupled to a single client's feature set or package path.
